title: M4 Target-Repo Product Refactor Plan
purpose: Recenter the public package on practical target-repository onboarding and remove architecture self-check surfaces that only preserve historical self-dogfood gates.
principles: Target repo owner value comes before source self-dogfood evidence.
Public CLI commands should be small, explainable, and useful without knowing this repository's history.
Historical architecture gates may remain as internal checks, but should not block target-repo product work.
Runtime code should prefer explicit constants and frozen enum-like maps over repeated string literals.
Release checks should protect package safety, command compatibility, and target-repo behavior rather than every retired architecture gate.

target_repo_feature_priorities.title: Target repo doctor
target_repo_feature_priorities.intent: Report onboarding readiness, detected stack, missing authority files, work item health, and boundary risks in one command.

target_repo_feature_priorities.title: Target onboarding repair
target_repo_feature_priorities.intent: Plan and optionally write missing canonical onboarding files without overwriting divergent user files by default.

target_repo_feature_priorities.title: Target repo profile detection
target_repo_feature_priorities.intent: Detect package managers, languages, docs surfaces, likely test commands, and CI files without running managed project commands.

target_repo_feature_priorities.title: Practical work item UX
target_repo_feature_priorities.intent: Add concise next/mine/summary views for humans and agents while preserving JSON output for automation.

target_repo_feature_priorities.title: Human-readable output mode
target_repo_feature_priorities.intent: Add explicit text output for target repo operators while keeping JSON as the stable automation contract.

architecture_refactor_priorities: Replace scattered command, status, schema, mode, and path literals with local frozen enum-like constants.
Split target-repo behavior out of runtime-composer into focused target, authority, guard, work-items, and release services.
Shrink or delete static architecture catalog sections that only validate retired gates.
Keep package allowlist and artifact boundary checks, but remove retired architecture gates from release --check.
Move any remaining historical self-dogfood assertions to agent-onboard-internal checks instead of the public package.
acceptance_criteria: P1S3M3 remains closed and P1S3M4 remains open without architecture --check failing solely because M3 is no longer open.
M4 work starts from target-repo product commands rather than new architecture gate commands.
Public release checks no longer require retired architecture parity smoke to pass.
Work-items runtime service uses constants for command family, paths, flags, modes, statuses, and result schemas.
The next implementation work item is a target-repo feature, not another self-dogfood architecture checker.
